A large service was discovered when undertaking the UTX. ~ Under the sleeper end of the down Sutton 6ft rail was a 9 inch thick brick wall. ~ Under the up Sutton 4 foot another 9 inch thick brick wall. ~ Spanning this is a 7 ½ inch notched out timber sleepers. These appear to be running parallel to the tracks. ~ The depths from the bottom of rail to the top of timber are 760mm. ~ When 1 of the timbers was removed underneath was full of consolidated silt. A decision was made not to expose the cable due to the possible deterioration of the cable within the silt.   ~ The service was traced with the RD 4000 and cable generator; this seems to run parallel to the tracks in the down mileage direction, it was traced for approx 25m. in the up mileage direction it was traced for 15m approx when it changed direction and crossed the down Sutton into the cess.

A large service was discovered when undertaking the UTX. ~ Under the sleeper end of the down Sutton 6ft rail was a 9 inch thick brick wall. ~ Under the up Sutton 4 foot another 9 inch thick brick wall. ~ Spanning this is a 7 ½ inch notched out timber sleepers. These appear to be running parallel to the tracks. ~ The depths from the bottom of rail to the top of timber are 760mm. ~ When 1 of the timbers was removed underneath was full of consolidated silt. A decision was made not to expose the cable due to the possible deterioration of the cable within the silt.   ~ The service was traced with the RD 4000 and cable generator; this seems to run parallel to the tracks in the down mileage direction, it was traced for approx 25m. in the up mileage direction it was traced for 15m approx when it changed direction and crossed the down Sutton into the cess.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: All tracks have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified line.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Requirement to disturb the surface within the station's controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator. It is suspected that low voltage cables are located underground throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cable

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Birmingham New Street contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory. Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Birmingham Fault control.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Limited records of the drainage layout are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Limited records of water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that these would be present to most areas of the station, including platforms.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Limited records of hot water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: There are several areas of the station where there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin as rats, pigeons, mice. Contractors should be aware that poison might be put down at various locations.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: This station is classed as a sub-surface station at platform level. It should be noted that no smoking is allowed in any part of this station. All change of use; tenant and refurbishment works require the approval of the Landlord and Network Rail Fire Advisor.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the Hinterland. The Boiler Room contains Gas, Hot/Cold Water and Electricity services, which present potential hazards of explosion, asphyxiation, scolding and electrocution. It has a high ceiling and the boilers produce significant levels of noise and may cause a high Personal Dose if work was carried out over a protracted period. Pipes cross the walking access routes at approximately 1.8m resulting in a potential for head injuries.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the Hinterland. The High Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to constitute a Limited Access area and presents hazards relating to electrocution.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the Hinterland. The Medium Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to constitute a Limited Access area and presents hazards relating to electrocution. In addition there is a caged area containing transformer equipment which also constitutes hazards relating to electrocution.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the rear end of platform 12. The High Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to constitute a Limited Access area and presents hazards relating to electrocution.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the platforms. These Low Voltage Switch Rooms have Limited Access and extensive electrical equipment resulting in electrocution hazards. There is also cabling at height, which could lead to hazards from working at height and falling objects. All Platform switch rooms, except platforms 1 & 12 have access to a Confined Space under the adjacent escalators.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: end of platform 1B. A Backup Generator compound is situated at the end of platform 1B and fuel is stored in this area. The compound is accessed through a spiked fence and the standby generator is situated in a wooden enclosure on Platform Island 2-3. This consists of two small rooms with Limited Access housing the generator and fuel respectively.

ELR Name: RUGBY TV JNC - BIRMINGHAM NEW ST (112M-73CH)
Free Text: Located at: the end of platform 1B. A Project Block is situated, it is believed that this has listed building status. The building has been secured and access is now restricted due to the buildings integrity. The ground floor is near derelict in places and is currently extremely hazardous. Electrical hazards i.e. exposed wiring are common. In one section wooden steps lead to a ceiling void, which is strewn with wiring and likely to have a weak floor. At one end of the building it is believed that stables used to exist raising the possibility of Anthrax Spores being present.
